title: Singapore develops Asia’s first AI-based mobile app for shark and ray fin identification to combat illegal wildlife trade
summary: The post Singapore develops Asia’s first AI-based mobile app for shark and ray fin identification to combat illegal wildlife trade appeared first on The AI Blog.
content: Singapore develops Asia’s first AI-based mobile app for shark and ray fin identification to combat illegal wildlife trade Jun 8, 2022 | Singapore News Center Share on Facebook (opens new window) Share on LinkedIn (opens new window) Share on X (opens new window) Singapore National Parks Board, Microsoft and Conservation International collaborate to create AI-powered mobile application that visually identifies shark and ray species Sharks and rays are in rapid decline. This joint innovation aims to combat a key driver of this decline; illegal wildlife trade Singapore, 8 June 2022 – The Singapore National Parks Board (NParks), Microsoft and Conservation International announced the launch of Fin Finder, Asia’s first mobile application that employs artificial intelligence (AI) to visually identify illegally traded shark and ray species. Through the tripartite collaboration, the mobile app was created by a Singapore-led team from Conservation International in consultation with NParks with support from the Microsoft AI for Earth program. The app will be used by officers from the Singapore National Parks Board to combat illegal wildlife trade. YouTube Video Click here to load media According to the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora (CITES) Appendix II [1] , there are approximately 1,000 species of sharks and rays in the world, of which over 30 species are listed under CITES Appendix II for regulated trade. In Singapore, more than 160,000 kilograms of fins from CITES-listed sharks and rays have entered the borders between 2012 and 2020 [2] . The current process requires officers to collect the fins from each shipment for DNA testing to determine its species. This takes an average of up to one week. In Singapore, more than 160,000 kilograms of fins from CITES-listed sharks and rays have entered the borders between 2012 and 2020. (Photo by: Microsoft, Conservation International and the Singapore National Parks Board) Fin Finder optimizes this process by allowing officers to take photos of fins that will be matched against a database of over 15,000 shark and ray fin images via an AI-driven algorithm in the app. In a matter of seconds, the AI-powered app which runs on Microsoft Azure will quickly and accurately provide a visual identification of shark and ray species onsite and empower officers to quickly flag suspicious fin shipments for further DNA testing to stop the illegal trade of shark and ray fins. Dhanushri Munasinghe, Project Coordinator, Conservation International Singapore said, “Sharks and rays play an important role in maintaining marine ecosystems by keeping other fish populations in check. If stripped from our oceans, there would be dire consequences for ocean health, which would affect us, and our food security. As one of the world’s most significant transhipment hubs, Singapore is well positioned to combat illegal wildlife trade. Conservation International, supported by Microsoft and other partners, is excited to support Singapore and the Singapore National Parks Board’s leadership in conservation through the creation of Fin Finder”. Dr Adrian Loo, Group Director of Wildlife Management, NParks, said: “When wildlife species are traded illegally, the consequences are far-reaching to many ecosystems, economies and communities around the world. By using advanced technology in the creation of Fin Finder, we can strengthen the enforcement against the illegal trade of sharks and ray species following CITES regulation, and boost Singapore’s capabilities in conserving precious biodiversity. The collaboration with Microsoft and Conservation International also reinforce the importance of collective efforts among the public and private sector in combating illegal wildlife trade.” Ivonne Higuero, Secretary-General of CITES , said: “The first step in ensuring international trade complies with CITES regulations comes with the, sometimes difficult, process of identifying the species being traded. Fin Finder is a welcome and innovative addition in the identification of fins and will complement other tools such as iSharkFin. It will give customs and enforcement officers an easy-to-use tool that will contribute to an international trade in CITES-listed species that remains legal, traceable, and sustainable.” Beyond identification of illegally traded shark and ray fins, officers from the Singapore National Parks Board will also use Fin Finder as a single-platform directory of relevant shark and ray species. The app also offers onsite access to reference materials that can be used for validation of CITES-approved permits or shipping documents. This feature is expected to reduce the time and effort spent to on shipment validation, enabling officers to help put a stop to illegal wildlife trade more quickly. A Singapore National Parks Board (NParks) inspector pilot tests Fin Finder on a shark fin to determine the species ID and other valuable information that helps determine the legality of the traded specimen. (Photo by: Microsoft, Conservation International and the Singapore National Parks Board) Fin Finder, a complex AI and cloud based mobile application that runs on Microsoft Azure, was created in just nine months to address a pressing need. The project was led by a Singapore-based team that was supported by a highly collaborative consortium of global experts in conservation and technology, with resources, data, and volunteer contributions from Microsoft, Conservation International, the Singapore National Parks Board, Sineurope Pte Ltd, Rumah Foundation, Coastal Natives and Wild Me. Richard Koh, Chief Technology Officer, Microsoft Singapore, shared, “AI has the potential to solve critical environmental challenges. By taking AI tools out of the lab and putting it into the hands of experts in the field, we can accelerate new solutions for a better world. That’s why we are proud to support Fin Finder as it protects global shark and ray populations and preserves our ocean life. By conserving wildlife with help from technology, future generations can enjoy our natural world, as we empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more.” Fin Finder is a project in Microsoft AI for Earth, a global program that supports organizations applying responsible AI and cloud computing to address critical environmental issues. The program is part of Microsoft’s AI for Good initiative, which aims to solve the world’s most challenging problems, from climate change to agriculture, biodiversity and water. To date, AI for Earth has awarded 138 grants to people and businesses in more than 45 countries globally. Learn more about Fin Finder here and here . [1] 39 CITES Appendix II, IUCN Shark Specialist Group News, Jan 2022 issue, pg. 16 [2] Extracted from the CITES Trade Database ( https://trade.cites.org/ ) Related Stories May 26, 2025 | Singapore News Center HTX inks contract with Mistral AI and Microsoft to boost AI model development for Home Team Apr 29, 2025 | Singapore News Center Microsoft Releases 2025 Work Trend Index: The Frontier Firm Emerges in Singapore Mar 11, 2025 | Singapore News Center Microsoft Expands AI Pinnacle Program with Public and Private Sector Collaborations for AI Adoption at Scale in Singapore Feb 26, 2025 | Singapore News Center AI adoption in law firms across Asia: Overcoming wariness to reap the benefits
aiSummary: 新加坡国家公园局、微软和国际保护组织合作，推出了亚洲首款基于人工智能的鲨鱼和鳐鱼鳍识别手机应用“Fin Finder”。该应用利用AI图像识别技术，可在几秒内快速识别物种，帮助执法人员现场筛查可疑的鱼鳍货物，打击非法野生动物贸易。此举旨在应对鲨鱼和鳐鱼数量锐减的危机，并提升新加坡作为重要转运港的执法能力。
aiInterpretation: 新加坡国家公园局、微软和国际保护组织联合推出了亚洲首款用于识别鲨鱼和鳐鱼鳍的人工智能手机应用“Fin Finder”。该应用由新加坡团队主导开发，并获得微软“AI for Earth”计划支持。它允许执法人员现场拍摄鱼鳍照片，通过AI算法与超过1.5万张图像的数据进行比对，在几秒钟内提供视觉识别结果，从而快速标记可疑货物以进行进一步的DNA检测。 为什么重要：鲨鱼和鳐鱼对维持海洋生态平衡至关重要，但其种群正因非法贸易而急剧减少。新加坡作为全球重要的转运枢纽，在2012年至2020年间，有超过16万公斤受《濒危野生动植物种国际贸易公约》管制的鲨鱼和鳐鱼鳍进入其边境。传统的DNA检测方法平均需要一周时间，严重影响了执法效率。这款AI应用的推出，直接针对这一痛点，将识别时间从数天缩短至数秒，是技术赋能环境保护和执法行动的关键创新。 影响面： 1. 技术：应用了先进的计算机视觉AI算法和微软Azure云平台，展示了AI在非传统领域（如野生动物保护）解决实际问题的潜力。 2. 商业：为科技公司（如微软）提供了将其AI和云服务应用于社会公益项目的成功案例，提升了企业社会责任形象，并可能催生类似的技术解决方案市场。 3. 产业链：直接针对非法野生动物贸易链条中的关键环节——物种识别与通关检查，通过提高查验效率和准确性，对非法贸易产业链形成有效威慑和打击。 4. 监管/安全：极大增强了海关和野生动物管理部门的执法能力，使基于《濒危野生动植物种国际贸易公约》的贸易监管更加高效、精准，有助于维护国际贸易的法律性和可持续性。 5. 用户/社会：最终用户（执法人员）获得了强大易用的现场工具，提升了工作效能。长远来看，保护海洋顶级捕食者有助于维护海洋生态健康，保障全球食物安全，惠及整个社会。
aiExplanation: 简单来说，新加坡开发了一个手机APP，海关人员用手机拍一下鱼鳍照片，AI就能快速判断这是不是受保护、不能非法买卖的鲨鱼或鳐鱼，就像给野生动物执法装上了‘火眼金睛’。
aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
新加坡联合科技公司与环保组织，推出了一个用手机AI快速识别非法鱼翅的应用。

[翻成人话]
海关人员以后查鱼翅，不用再等漫长的DNA检测了，用手机拍个照，AI几秒钟就能告诉你这鱼翅是不是来自受保护的鲨鱼或鳐鱼。

[为什么重要]
因为非法鱼翅贸易是导致鲨鱼、鳐鱼数量锐减的主要原因之一，而新加坡是全球重要的海运枢纽，堵住这个关口对保护海洋生态至关重要。这个应用能极大提升查验效率，精准打击非法贸易。

[下一步确认点]
这个AI应用的识别准确率在实际大规模使用中表现如何，以及未来是否会向其他国家和地区的执法机构推广使用。
fullText: Singapore develops Asia’s first AI-based mobile app for shark and ray fin identification to combat illegal wildlife trade Jun 8, 2022 | Singapore News Center Share on Facebook (opens new window) Share on LinkedIn (opens new window) Share on X (opens new window) Singapore National Parks Board, Microsoft and Conservation International collaborate to create AI-powered mobile application that visually identifies shark and ray species Sharks and rays are in rapid decline. This joint innovation aims to combat a key driver of this decline; illegal wildlife trade Singapore, 8 June 2022 – The Singapore National Parks Board (NParks), Microsoft and Conservation International announced the launch of Fin Finder, Asia’s first mobile application that employs artificial intelligence (AI) to visually identify illegally traded shark and ray species. Through the tripartite collaboration, the mobile app was created by a Singapore-led team from Conservation International in consultation with NParks with support from the Microsoft AI for Earth program. The app will be used by officers from the Singapore National Parks Board to combat illegal wildlife trade. YouTube Video Click here to load media According to the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora (CITES) Appendix II [1] , there are approximately 1,000 species of sharks and rays in the world, of which over 30 species are listed under CITES Appendix II for regulated trade. In Singapore, more than 160,000 kilograms of fins from CITES-listed sharks and rays have entered the borders between 2012 and 2020 [2] . The current process requires officers to collect the fins from each shipment for DNA testing to determine its species. This takes an average of up to one week. In Singapore, more than 160,000 kilograms of fins from CITES-listed sharks and rays have entered the borders between 2012 and 2020. (Photo by: Microsoft, Conservation International and the Singapore National Parks Board) Fin Finder optimizes this process by allowing officers to take photos of fins that will be matched against a database of over 15,000 shark and ray fin images via an AI-driven algorithm in the app. In a matter of seconds, the AI-powered app which runs on Microsoft Azure will quickly and accurately provide a visual identification of shark and ray species onsite and empower officers to quickly flag suspicious fin shipments for further DNA testing to stop the illegal trade of shark and ray fins. Dhanushri Munasinghe, Project Coordinator, Conservation International Singapore said, “Sharks and rays play an important role in maintaining marine ecosystems by keeping other fish populations in check. If stripped from our oceans, there would be dire consequences for ocean health, which would affect us, and our food security. As one of the world’s most significant transhipment hubs, Singapore is well positioned to combat illegal wildlife trade. Conservation International, supported by Microsoft and other partners, is excited to support Singapore and the Singapore National Parks Board’s leadership in conservation through the creation of Fin Finder”. Dr Adrian Loo, Group Director of Wildlife Management, NParks, said: “When wildlife species are traded illegally, the consequences are far-reaching to many ecosystems, economies and communities around the world. By using advanced technology in the creation of Fin Finder, we can strengthen the enforcement against the illegal trade of sharks and ray species following CITES regulation, and boost Singapore’s capabilities in conserving precious biodiversity. The collaboration with Microsoft and Conservation International also reinforce the importance of collective efforts among the public and private sector in combating illegal wildlife trade.” Ivonne Higuero, Secretary-General of CITES , said: “The first step in ensuring international trade complies with CITES regulations comes with the, sometimes difficult, process of identifying the species being traded. Fin Finder is a welcome and innovative addition in the identification of fins and will complement other tools such as iSharkFin. It will give customs and enforcement officers an easy-to-use tool that will contribute to an international trade in CITES-listed species that remains legal, traceable, and sustainable.” Beyond identification of illegally traded shark and ray fins, officers from the Singapore National Parks Board will also use Fin Finder as a single-platform directory of relevant shark and ray species. The app also offers onsite access to reference materials that can be used for validation of CITES-approved permits or shipping documents. This feature is expected to reduce the time and effort spent to on shipment validation, enabling officers to help put a stop to illegal wildlife trade more quickly. A Singapore National Parks Board (NParks) inspector pilot tests Fin Finder on a shark fin to determine the species ID and other valuable information that helps determine the legality of the traded specimen. (Photo by: Microsoft, Conservation International and the Singapore National Parks Board) Fin Finder, a complex AI and cloud based mobile application that runs on Microsoft Azure, was created in just nine months to address a pressing need. The project was led by a Singapore-based team that was supported by a highly collaborative consortium of global experts in conservation and technology, with resources, data, and volunteer contributions from Microsoft, Conservation International, the Singapore National Parks Board, Sineurope Pte Ltd, Rumah Foundation, Coastal Natives and Wild Me. Richard Koh, Chief Technology Officer, Microsoft Singapore, shared, “AI has the potential to solve critical environmental challenges. By taking AI tools out of the lab and putting it into the hands of experts in the field, we can accelerate new solutions for a better world. That’s why we are proud to support Fin Finder as it protects global shark and ray populations and preserves our ocean life. By conserving wildlife with help from technology, future generations can enjoy our natural world, as we empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more.” Fin Finder is a project in Microsoft AI for Earth, a global program that supports organizations applying responsible AI and cloud computing to address critical environmental issues. The program is part of Microsoft’s AI for Good initiative, which aims to solve the world’s most challenging problems, from climate change to agriculture, biodiversity and water. To date, AI for Earth has awarded 138 grants to people and businesses in more than 45 countries globally. Learn more about Fin Finder here and here . [1] 39 CITES Appendix II, IUCN Shark Specialist Group News, Jan 2022 issue, pg. 16 [2] Extracted from the CITES Trade Database ( https://trade.cites.org/ ) Related Stories May 26, 2025 | Singapore News Center HTX inks contract with Mistral AI and Microsoft to boost AI model development for Home Team Apr 29, 2025 | Singapore News Center Microsoft Releases 2025 Work Trend Index: The Frontier Firm Emerges in Singapore Mar 11, 2025 | Singapore News Center Microsoft Expands AI Pinnacle Program with Public and Private Sector Collaborations for AI Adoption at Scale in Singapore Feb 26, 2025 | Singapore News Center AI adoption in law firms across Asia: Overcoming wariness to reap the benefits

title: The opportunity at home – can AI drive innovation in personal assistant devices and sign language?
summary: The post The opportunity at home – can AI drive innovation in personal assistant devices and sign language? appeared first on The AI Blog.
content: AI for Accessibility The opportunity at home – can AI drive innovation in personal assistant devices and sign language? Ioana Tanase May 31, 2022 Share on X (opens new window) Share on Facebook (opens new window) Share on LinkedIn (opens new window) Share on Flipboard (opens new window) Advancing tech innovation and combating the data dessert that exists related to sign language have been areas of focus for the AI for Accessibility program. Towards those goals, in 2019 the team hosted a sign language workshop, soliciting applications from top researchers in the field. Abraham Glasser, a Ph.D. student in Computing and Information Sciences and a native American Sign Language (ASL) signer, supervised by Professor Matt Huenerfauth, was awarded a three-year grant. His work would focus on a very pragmatic need and opportunity: driving inclusion by concentrating on and improving common interactions with home-based smart assistants for people who use sign language as a primary form of communication. Since then, faculty and students in the Golisano College of Computing and Information Sciences at Rochester Institute of Technology (RIT) conducted the work at the Center for Accessibility and Inclusion Research (CAIR). CAIR publishes research on computing accessibility and it includes many Deaf and Hard of Hearing (DHH) students operating bilingually in English and American Sign Language. To begin this research, the team investigated how DHH users would optimally prefer to interact with their personal assistant devices, be it a smart speaker other type of devices in the household that respond to spoken command. Traditionally, these devices have used voice-based interaction, and as technology evolved, newer models now incorporate cameras and display screens. Currently, none of the available devices on the market understand commands in ASL or other sign languages, so introducing that capability is an important future tech development to address an untapped customer base and drive inclusion. Abraham explored simulated scenarios in which, through the camera on the device, the tech would be able to watch the signing of a user, process their request, and display the output result on the screen of the device. Some prior research had focused on the phases of interacting with a personal assistant device, but little included DHH users. Some examples of available research included studying device activation, including the concerns of waking up a device, as well as device output modalities in the form for videos, ASL avatars and English captions. The call to action from a research perspective included collecting more data, the key bottleneck, for sign language technologies. To pave the way forward for technological advancements it was critical to understand what DHH users would like the interaction with the devices to look like and what type of commands they would like to issue. Abraham and the team set up a Wizard-of-Oz videoconferencing setup. A “wizard” ASL interpreter had a home personal assistant device in the room with them, joining the call without being seen on camera. The device’s screen and output would be viewable in the call’s video window and each participant was guided by a research moderator. As the Deaf participants signed to the personal home device, they did not know that the ASL interpreter was voicing the commands in spoken English. A team of annotators watched the recording, identifying key segments of the videos, and transcribing each command into English and ASL gloss. Abraham was able to identify new ways that users would interact with the device, such as “wake-up” commands which were not captured in previous research. Screenshots of various “wake up” signs produced by participants during the study conducted remotely by researchers from the Rochester Institute of Technology. Participants were interacting with a personal assistant device, using American Sign Language (ASL) commands which were translated by an unseen ASL interpreter, and they spontaneously used a variety of ASL signs to activate the personal assistant device before giving each command. The signs here include examples labeled as: (a) HELLO, (b) HEY, (c) HI, (d) CURIOUS, (e) DO-DO, and (f) A-L-E-X-A. Additionally, a summarization of command categories and frequencies showed the most popular category was “command and control” where users adjust device settings, navigate through the results and answer yes/no style of questions. The next popular category was related to entertainment questions, followed by lifestyle and shopping. Furthermore, despite signing into a device, participants made sophisticated use of the spaces around their bodies, for example to represent and refer to people or things that were the topic of their questions. Another observation was the use of a question-mark sign at the beginning of yes or no questions, to call the attention of the device, while typically this sign more often used at the end of such questions. When it came to errors, such as the device not giving the result the users were looking for, most commonly users would simply ignore the error and proceed with a different command. A close second method was to repeat the command with the exact same wording and signing style, followed by rewording the command. For instance, some reworded their questions to be more English language like, or fingerspelling words for emphasis upon re-attempts. A paper with the full details of the research has been presented and published in the Proceedings of the 2022 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems, entitled “ Analyzing Deaf and Hard-of-Hearing Users’ Behavior, Usage, and Interaction with a Personal Assistant Device that Understands Sign-Language Input ” by Abraham Glasser, Matthew Watkins, Kira Hart, Sooyeon Lee, Matt Huenerfauth. The knowledge gained through this research was then the basis of building a video dataset of recording of DHH people producing commands in ASL and interacting with their personal assistant devices – such as asking about the weather, controlling electronics in their home environment, and more. In using surveys and interviews to gather preferences and requirements from DHH users, videos were collected of ASL commands, leading to the production of a publicly available dataset that can be further leveraged by the research community to train ASL recognition technologies. However, the dataset would be useful for developers of personal assistant technologies as well, and for developers and researchers investigating sign language technologies. While there are still many opportunities ahead to incorporate sign languages in tech, and innovate, the work Abraham and team have undertaken in the last three years represents an important milestone to further innovation in accessibility and ensure inclusion for all
aiSummary: 微软AI无障碍项目资助的一项研究探索了如何让智能家居助手理解手语。罗切斯特理工学院的研究团队通过实验，收集了聋哑及重听用户使用美国手语与设备交互的数据，并创建了首个公开的手语指令数据集。这项研究旨在解决手语技术面临的数据匮乏问题，为开发真正包容性的个人助理设备铺平道路。
aiInterpretation: 为什么重要：全球有数亿聋哑及重听人士，主流语音交互的智能设备将他们排除在外。这项研究直接针对这一“数据荒漠”，探索如何让个人助理设备理解手语输入，是推动科技普惠、实现真正数字包容的关键一步。 影响面： 1. 技术：研究创建了首个公开的美国手语指令视频数据集，为训练更精准的手语识别AI模型提供了宝贵资源，突破了该领域长期存在的数据瓶颈。 2. 商业：为科技公司开辟了一个尚未被充分服务的庞大用户市场，智能家居、个人助理设备有望通过增加手语功能获得新的增长点。 3. 用户/社会：直接惠及聋哑及重听社区，让他们能平等、便捷地使用智能家居技术，提升独立生活能力和生活质量，是科技向善的体现。 4. 产业链：将推动从传感器（如摄像头）到算法，再到应用开发的全产业链对手语交互功能的重视与投入，可能催生新的软硬件解决方案。 5. 监管/安全：随着此类技术发展，如何确保手语数据采集的隐私性、识别算法的公平性与无偏见，将成为未来需要关注的监管与伦理议题。
aiExplanation: 简单说，就是科学家们正在教智能音箱、智能家居“看懂”手语，让不方便说话的人也能用声音控制一样方便地指挥它们。这就像给机器配了个手语翻译。

aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
微软资助的科研团队，成功完成了让智能设备“看懂”美国手语的初步研究，并发布了首个相关数据集。

[翻成人话]
科学家们想办法，让像智能音箱这样的设备，不用听你说话，而是通过摄像头看你的手语手势，就能明白你想让它干什么。

[为什么重要]
因为这能让全球数亿聋哑人士也能轻松享受智能科技的便利，比如问天气、控制家电，是科技走向真正平等包容的一大步。

[下一步确认点]
接下来要看科技公司是否会利用这个数据集，真正开发出能理解手语的消费级产品，并解决用户隐私和数据安全等实际问题。

title: Pacific Northwest National Laboratory and OpenAI partner to accelerate federal permitting
summary: OpenAI and Pacific Northwest National Laboratory introduce DraftNEPABench, a new benchmark evaluating how AI coding agents can accelerate federal permitting—showing potential to reduce NEPA drafting time by up to 15% and modernize infrastructure reviews.
content: OpenAI and Pacific Northwest National Laboratory introduce DraftNEPABench, a new benchmark evaluating how AI coding agents can accelerate federal permitting—showing potential to reduce NEPA drafting time by up to 15% and modernize infrastructure reviews.
aiSummary: 美国能源部下属的太平洋西北国家实验室与OpenAI合作，推出了名为DraftNEPABench的新基准测试。该测试旨在评估AI编码助手如何加速联邦环境许可流程，初步结果显示，AI工具有潜力将《国家环境政策法》相关文件的起草时间缩短高达15%，从而推动基础设施项目审查的现代化。
aiInterpretation: 美国能源部下属的太平洋西北国家实验室与人工智能公司OpenAI近日宣布合作，共同推出了名为DraftNEPABench的新基准测试。该测试的核心目标是评估AI编码助手在加速联邦环境许可流程方面的能力，特别是针对《国家环境政策法》要求的复杂文件起草工作。初步研究显示，AI工具有潜力将NEPA相关文件的起草时间缩短高达15%。 为什么重要：联邦环境许可流程，尤其是NEPA审查，通常耗时数年，是大型基础设施项目（如电网升级、可再生能源设施建设）的主要瓶颈。加速这一流程对于实现国家气候目标和推动关键基础设施建设至关重要。AI的介入有望显著提升效率，减少项目延误。 影响面： 1. 技术：该基准测试推动了AI在特定、复杂的法律与政策文件生成领域的应用技术发展，测试了AI处理结构化要求和大量背景信息的能力。 2. 商业：对于工程、建筑和能源开发公司而言，缩短许可时间意味着项目能更快启动和完成，降低资金成本和不确定性，提升投资回报率。 3. 产业链：加速基础设施审批将利好从原材料供应、工程建设到后期运营的整个产业链，尤其能促进清洁能源和交通等关键领域的发展。 4. 监管/安全：AI辅助起草需要确保生成内容的准确性、合规性，并符合环保标准，这引发了关于如何监管AI在关键政府流程中应用、以及如何划分人机责任的新讨论。 5. 用户/社会：最终，更快的项目审批意味着公众能更早受益于升级的电网、更清洁的能源和更完善的交通设施，有助于整体社会福利和经济竞争力的提升。
aiExplanation: 简单说，就是国家实验室和AI公司合作，测试用人工智能来帮忙写政府环保审批文件。如果成功，能让修路、建电站这类大项目的审批速度快不少，省时省钱。
aiBroadcastScript:
[这条是什么]
太平洋西北国家实验室和OpenAI合作，搞了个新测试，看AI能不能帮政府更快地批环保许可。

[翻成人话]
他们弄了个“AI写公文模拟考”，专攻最耗时的那种环境评估报告。考完发现，AI可能帮公务员省下15%的写报告时间。

[为什么重要]
因为现在美国搞基建，比如修新能源电站或升级电网，光等环保批复就要好几年，太慢了。用AI提提速，项目就能早上马，对国家实现减排目标和经济发展都关键。

[下一步确认点]
接下来得看：1. AI写的报告质量到底过不过关，会不会出错。2. 政府监管部门认不认AI辅助生成的文书。3. 这个工具啥时候能真正给公务员用上。
fullText: OpenAI and Pacific Northwest National Laboratory introduce DraftNEPABench, a new benchmark evaluating how AI coding agents can accelerate federal permitting—showing potential to reduce NEPA drafting time by up to 15% and modernize infrastructure reviews

title: What’s new with GitHub Copilot coding agent
summary:
GitHub Copilot coding agent now includes a model picker, self-review, built-in security scanning, custom agents, and CLI handoff. Here's what's new and how to use it.

The post What’s new with GitHub Copilot coding agent appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
content: Home / AI & ML / GitHub Copilot What’s new with GitHub Copilot coding agent GitHub Copilot coding agent now includes a model picker, self-review, built-in security scanning, custom agents, and CLI handoff. Here’s what’s new and how to use it. Andrea Griffiths · @AndreaGriffiths11 February 26, 2026 | 5 minutes Share: You open an issue before lunch. By the time you’re back, there’s a pull request waiting. That’s what GitHub Copilot coding agent is built for. It works in the background, fixing bugs, adding tests, cleaning up debt, and comes back with a pull request when it’s done. While you’re writing code in your editor with Copilot in real time, the coding agent is handling the work you’ve delegated. A few recent updates make that handoff more useful. Here’s what shipped and how to start using it. Visual learner? Watch the video above! ☝️ Choose the right model for each task The Agents panel now includes a model picker. Before, every background task ran on a single default model. You couldn’t pay for a more robust model to complete harder work or prioritize speed on routine tasks. Now you can. Use a faster model for straightforward work like adding unit tests. Upgrade your model for a gnarly refactor or integration tests with real edge cases. If you’d rather not think about it, leave it on auto. To get started: Open the Agents panel (top-right in GitHub), select your repo, and pick a model. Write a clear prompt and kick off the task. Leave the model on auto if you’d rather let GitHub choose. Model selection is available for Copilot Pro and Pro+ users now, with support for Business and Enterprise coming soon. Learn more about model selection with Copilot coding agent. 👉 Pull requests that arrive in better shape The painful part of reviewing agent output has always been the cleanup. You open the diff and there it is: logic that technically works, but nobody would write it that way. Copilot coding agent now reviews its own changes using Copilot code review before it opens the pull request. It gets feedback, iterates, and improves the patch. By the time you’re tagged for review, someone already went through it. In one session , the agent caught that its own string concatenation was overly complex and fixed it before the pull request landed. That kind of thing used to be your problem. To get started: Assign an issue to Copilot or create a task from the Agents panel. Click into the task to view the logs. See the moments where the agent ran Copilot code review and applied feedback. Review the pull request when prompted. Copilot requests your review only after it has iterated. Learn more about Copilot code review + Copilot coding agent. 👉 Security checks that run while the agent works Just like with human-generated code, AI-generated code can introduce real risks: vulnerable patterns, secrets accidentally committed, dependencies with known CVEs. The difference is it does it faster. And you really don’t want to find that in review. Copilot coding agent now runs code scanning, secret scanning, and dependency vulnerability checks directly inside its workflow. If a dependency has a known issue, or something looks like a committed API key, it gets flagged before the pull request opens. Code scanning is normally part of GitHub Advanced Security. With Copilot coding agent, you get it for free. To get started: Run any task through the Agents panel. Check the session logs as it runs. You’ll see scanning entries as the agent works. Review the pull request. It’s already been through the security filter. Learn more about security scanning in Copilot coding agent. 👉 Custom agents that follow your team’s process A short prompt leaves a lot to judgment. And that judgment isn’t always consistent with how your team actually works. Custom agents let you codify it. Create a file under .github/agents/ and define a specific approach. A performance optimizer agent, for example, can be wired to benchmark first, make the change, then measure the difference before opening a pull request. In a recent GitHub Checkout demo , that’s exactly what happened. The agent benchmarked a lookup, made a targeted fix, and came back with a 99% improvement on that one function. Small scope, real data, no guessing. You can share custom agents across your org or enterprise too, so the same process applies everywhere teams are using the coding agent. To get started: Create an agent file under .github/agents/ in your repo. Open the Agents panel and start a new task. Select your custom agent from the options. Write a prompt scoped to what that agent does. Learn more about creating custom agents. 👉 Move between cloud and local without losing context Sometimes you start something in the cloud and want to finish it locally. Sometimes you’re deep in your terminal and want to hand something off without losing your flow. Either way, switching contexts used to mean starting the conversation over. Now it doesn’t. Pull a cloud session into your terminal and you get the branch, the logs, and the full context. Or press & in the CLI to push work back to the cloud and keep going on your end. To get started: Start a task with Copilot coding agent and wait for the session to appear. Click “Continue in Copilot CLI” and copy the command. Paste it in your terminal to load the session locally with branch, logs, and context intact. Press the ampersand symbol ( & ) in the CLI to delegate work back to the cloud and keep going locally. Learn more about Copilot coding agent + CLI handoff. 👉 What this adds up to Copilot coding agent has come a long way. Model selection, self-review, security scanning, custom agents, CLI handoff—and that’s just what shipped recently. The team is actively working on private mode, planning before coding, and using the agent for things that don’t even need a pull request, like summarizing issues or generating reports. There’s a lot more coming. Stay tuned. Share feedback on what ships next in GitHub Community discussions . aiSummary: GitHub Copilot 编程助手迎来多项重要更新，旨在提升AI辅助编程的效率和可靠性。新功能包括模型选择器，允许用户为不同任务匹配合适的AI模型；自我审查机制，让AI在提交代码前自行优化；内置安全扫描，实时检测漏洞和敏感信息泄露；自定义智能体，可固化团队开发流程；以及云与本地CLI的无缝切换，保持工作上下文连贯。这些更新主要面向Copilot Pro及以上用户，旨在让AI助手更智能、更安全、更贴合实际工作流。
aiInterpretation: GitHub Copilot 编程助手近期发布了一系列核心更新，显著增强了其作为AI编程伙伴的能力。本次更新包含五大关键功能：模型选择器、自我审查、内置安全扫描、自定义智能体以及CLI无缝切换。 为什么重要？ 这些更新标志着AI编程工具从基础的代码补全，向更自主、更可靠、更可定制的“智能协作者”演进。它不再仅仅是反应式的工具，而是能主动处理复杂任务、遵循安全规范、并适配团队独特流程的代理。这降低了开发者在代码审查、安全审计和流程一致性上的心智负担，将AI更深地集成到软件开发生命周期中。 影响面： 1. 技术：引入了模型选择和自我审查，提升了AI生成代码的质量和针对性，使技术决策更精细化。 2. 商业：通过提供差异化功能（如高级安全扫描），增强了Copilot产品套件对专业和企业级用户的吸引力，可能推动订阅转化和收入增长。 3. 产业链：自定义智能体功能允许固化最佳实践，可能促进开发流程标准化，影响DevOps工具链的整合方式。 4. 监管/安全：内置的自动化安全扫描（代码、密钥、依赖漏洞）将安全左移，直接在AI工作流中拦截风险，有助于满足日益严格的安全与合规要求。 5. 用户/社会：提升了开发者的工作效率与代码质量，可能改变开发者的工作模式，使其更专注于高价值设计而非重复性任务，同时也对开发者的提示词工程和AI工作流管理能力提出了新要求。
aiExplanation: 简单说，GitHub的AI编程助手变得更聪明、更懂事了。它能自己选最合适的“大脑”干活，写完代码还会自己先检查修改，干活时自动做安全检查，还能被训练成遵循你公司流程的专属助手，并且在电脑和云端之间切换工作不掉线。

[这条是什么]
GitHub Copilot编程助手推出五大新功能，让AI写代码更靠谱。

[翻成人话]
AI编程小助手升级了：1) 能根据活儿的难易选不同的AI模型；2) 提交代码前会自己先改一遍；3) 边写边自动做安全检查；4) 可以训练成按你团队规矩办事的专属助手；5) 在云端和本地电脑之间切换工作，不用从头再来。

这些新功能目前主要面向付费的Pro和Pro+用户，企业和团队版即将支持。想用的话，得先看看自己的订阅等级，然后去GitHub的Agents面板里体验。
fullText: Home / AI & ML / GitHub Copilot What’s new with GitHub Copilot coding agent GitHub Copilot coding agent now includes a model picker, self-review, built-in security scanning, custom agents, and CLI handoff. Here’s what’s new and how to use it. Andrea Griffiths · @AndreaGriffiths11 February 26, 2026 | 5 minutes Share: You open an issue before lunch. By the time you’re back, there’s a pull request waiting. That’s what GitHub Copilot coding agent is built for. It works in the background, fixing bugs, adding tests, cleaning up debt, and comes back with a pull request when it’s done. While you’re writing code in your editor with Copilot in real time, the coding agent is handling the work you’ve delegated. A few recent updates make that handoff more useful. Here’s what shipped and how to start using it. Visual learner? Watch the video above! ☝️ Choose the right model for each task The Agents panel now includes a model picker. Before, every background task ran on a single default model. You couldn’t pay for a more robust model to complete harder work or prioritize speed on routine tasks. Now you can. Use a faster model for straightforward work like adding unit tests. Upgrade your model for a gnarly refactor or integration tests with real edge cases. If you’d rather not think about it, leave it on auto. To get started: Open the Agents panel (top-right in GitHub), select your repo, and pick a model. Write a clear prompt and kick off the task. Leave the model on auto if you’d rather let GitHub choose. Model selection is available for Copilot Pro and Pro+ users now, with support for Business and Enterprise coming soon. Learn more about model selection with Copilot coding agent. 👉 Pull requests that arrive in better shape The painful part of reviewing agent output has always been the cleanup. You open the diff and there it is: logic that technically works, but nobody would write it that way. Copilot coding agent now reviews its own changes using Copilot code review before it opens the pull request. It gets feedback, iterates, and improves the patch. By the time you’re tagged for review, someone already went through it. In one session , the agent caught that its own string concatenation was overly complex and fixed it before the pull request landed. That kind of thing used to be your problem. To get started: Assign an issue to Copilot or create a task from the Agents panel. Click into the task to view the logs. See the moments where the agent ran Copilot code review and applied feedback. Review the pull request when prompted. Copilot requests your review only after it has iterated. Learn more about Copilot code review + Copilot coding agent. 👉 Security checks that run while the agent works Just like with human-generated code, AI-generated code can introduce real risks: vulnerable patterns, secrets accidentally committed, dependencies with known CVEs. The difference is it does it faster. And you really don’t want to find that in review. Copilot coding agent now runs code scanning, secret scanning, and dependency vulnerability checks directly inside its workflow. If a dependency has a known issue, or something looks like a committed API key, it gets flagged before the pull request opens. Code scanning is normally part of GitHub Advanced Security. With Copilot coding agent, you get it for free. To get started: Run any task through the Agents panel. Check the session logs as it runs. You’ll see scanning entries as the agent works. Review the pull request. It’s already been through the security filter. Learn more about security scanning in Copilot coding agent. 👉 Custom agents that follow your team’s process A short prompt leaves a lot to judgment. And that judgment isn’t always consistent with how your team actually works. Custom agents let you codify it. Create a file under .github/agents/ and define a specific approach. A performance optimizer agent, for example, can be wired to benchmark first, make the change, then measure the difference before opening a pull request. In a recent GitHub Checkout demo , that’s exactly what happened. The agent benchmarked a lookup, made a targeted fix, and came back with a 99% improvement on that one function. Small scope, real data, no guessing. You can share custom agents across your org or enterprise too, so the same process applies everywhere teams are using the coding agent. To get started: Create an agent file under .github/agents/ in your repo. Open the Agents panel and start a new task. Select your custom agent from the options. Write a prompt scoped to what that agent does. Learn more about creating custom agents. 👉 Move between cloud and local without losing context Sometimes you start something in the cloud and want to finish it locally. Sometimes you’re deep in your terminal and want to hand something off without losing your flow. Either way, switching contexts used to mean starting the conversation over. Now it doesn’t. Pull a cloud session into your terminal and you get the branch, the logs, and the full context. Or press & in the CLI to push work back to the cloud and keep going on your end. To get started: Start a task with Copilot coding agent and wait for the session to appear. Click “Continue in Copilot CLI” and copy the command. Paste it in your terminal to load the session locally with branch, logs, and context intact. Press the ampersand symbol ( & ) in the CLI to delegate work back to the cloud and keep going locally. Learn more about Copilot coding agent + CLI handoff. 👉 What this adds up to Copilot coding agent has come a long way. Model selection, self-review, security scanning, custom agents, CLI handoff—and that’s just what shipped recently. The team is actively working on private mode, planning before coding, and using the agent for things that don’t even need a pull request, like summarizing issues or generating reports. There’s a lot more coming. Stay tuned. Share feedback on what ships next in GitHub Community discussions . title: Graph Your Way to Inspiration: Integrating Co-Author Graphs with Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Large Language Model Based Scientific Idea Generation
summary:
arXiv:2602.22215v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) demonstrate potential in the field of scientific idea generation. However, the generated results often lack controllable academic context and traceable inspiration pathways. To bridge this gap, this paper proposes a scientific idea generation system called GYWI, which combines author knowledge graphs with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to form an external knowledge base to provide controllable context and trace o
content:
arXiv:2602.22215v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) demonstrate potential in the field of scientific idea generation. However, the generated results often lack controllable academic context and traceable inspiration pathways. To bridge this gap, this paper proposes a scientific idea generation system called GYWI, which combines author knowledge graphs with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to form an external knowledge base to provide controllable context and trace of inspiration path for LLMs to generate new scientific ideas. We first propose an author-centered knowledge graph construction method and inspiration source sampling algorithms to construct external knowledge base. Then, we propose a hybrid retrieval mechanism that is composed of both RAG and GraphRAG to retrieve content with both depth and breadth knowledge. It forms a hybrid context. Thirdly, we propose a Prompt optimization strategy incorporating reinforcement learning principles to automatically guide LLMs optimizing the results based on the hybrid context. To evaluate the proposed approaches, we constructed an evaluation dataset based on arXiv (2018-2023). This paper also develops a comprehensive evaluation method including empirical automatic assessment in multiple-choice question task, LLM-based scoring, human evaluation, and semantic space visualization analysis. The generated ideas are evaluated from the following five dimensions: novelty, feasibility, clarity, relevance, and significance. We conducted experiments on different LLMs including GPT-4o, DeepSeek-V3, Qwen3-8B, and Gemini 2.5. Experimental results show that GYWI significantly outperforms mainstream LLMs in multiple metrics such as novelty, reliability, and relevance.
aiSummary: 研究人员提出GYWI系统，将作者知识图谱与检索增强生成结合，为大型语言模型提供可控的学术背景和可追溯的灵感路径，以生成更优质的科学创意。该系统通过混合检索机制和强化学习优化提示策略，在多个评估维度上显著优于主流大模型。
aiInterpretation:
这篇论文提出了一种名为GYWI的科学创意生成系统，旨在解决大型语言模型在科学创意生成中缺乏可控学术背景和可追溯灵感路径的问题。GYWI通过整合作者知识图谱与检索增强生成技术，构建外部知识库，为LLMs提供结构化的学术上下文和灵感溯源支持。

系统的核心创新包括：首先，提出以作者为中心的知识图谱构建方法和灵感源采样算法，形成外部知识库；其次，设计混合检索机制，结合传统RAG和GraphRAG技术，实现深度与广度知识检索，形成混合上下文；第三，引入基于强化学习原理的提示优化策略，自动指导LLMs基于混合上下文优化生成结果。

为评估系统性能，研究团队基于arXiv（2018-2023）构建评估数据集，并开发了包含多项选择题任务实证评估、LLM评分、人工评估和语义空间可视化分析的综合评估方法。评估从新颖性、可行性、清晰度、相关性和重要性五个维度进行。实验在GPT-4o、DeepSeek-V3、Qwen3-8B和Gemini 2.5等多个LLMs上展开，结果显示GYWI在新颖性、可靠性和相关性等多个指标上显著优于主流LLMs。

这项研究的重要性在于为AI驱动的科学发现提供了更结构化、可追溯的方法框架，有望提升科研创新的系统性和可靠性，推动人工智能在科学研究中的深度应用。
aiExplanation: 用作者关系图辅助AI生成科研灵感，让想法更可控、有据可循，提升创新性与可靠性。

title: FIRE: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Financial Intelligence and Reasoning Evaluation
summary:
arXiv:2602.22273v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We introduce FIRE, a comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate both the theoretical financial knowledge of LLMs and their ability to handle practical business scenarios. For theoretical assessment, we curate a diverse set of examination questions drawn from widely recognized financial qualification exams, enabling evaluation of LLMs deep understanding and application of financial knowledge. In addition, to assess the practical value of LLMs in
content:
arXiv:2602.22273v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We introduce FIRE, a comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate both the theoretical financial knowledge of LLMs and their ability to handle practical business scenarios. For theoretical assessment, we curate a diverse set of examination questions drawn from widely recognized financial qualification exams, enabling evaluation of LLMs deep understanding and application of financial knowledge. In addition, to assess the practical value of LLMs in real-world financial tasks, we propose a systematic evaluation matrix that categorizes complex financial domains and ensures coverage of essential subdomains and business activities. Based on this evaluation matrix, we collect 3,000 financial scenario questions, consisting of closed-form decision questions with reference answers and open-ended questions evaluated by predefined rubrics. We conduct comprehensive evaluations of state-of-the-art LLMs on the FIRE benchmark, including XuanYuan 4.0, our latest financial-domain model, as a strong in-domain baseline. These results enable a systematic analysis of the capability boundaries of current LLMs in financial applications. We publicly release the benchmark questions and evaluation code to facilitate future research.
aiSummary: 研究者推出FIRE基准测试，全面评估大语言模型在金融领域的理论知识和实践能力。该测试包含3000个金融场景问题，涵盖资格考试题和实际业务场景。测试结果显示当前模型在金融应用中的能力边界，相关数据和代码已公开。
aiInterpretation: FIRE是一个综合性基准测试，旨在系统评估大语言模型在金融领域的理论知识和实践应用能力。该测试包含两大核心部分：理论评估采用来自权威金融资格考试的多样化题目，检验模型对金融知识的深度理解和应用；实践评估则通过系统化的评估矩阵，覆盖复杂金融领域的核心子领域和商业活动，收集了3000个金融场景问题，包括有标准答案的封闭式决策题和按预设标准评分的开放式问题。研究团队在FIRE上对包括其最新金融领域模型XuanYuan 4.0在内的先进大语言模型进行了全面评估，XuanYuan 4.0作为领域内强基线参与测试。这些评估结果有助于系统分析当前大语言模型在金融应用中的能力边界和局限性。为促进后续研究，团队已公开基准测试问题和评估代码。该工作对推动金融智能技术的发展具有重要意义，为模型在专业领域的性能评估提供了标准化框架。
aiExplanation: FIRE是一个金融AI能力测试，通过理论考试题和实际业务场景题，全面评估大模型在金融领域的知识水平和应用能力。

title: Multi-Level Causal Embeddings
summary:
arXiv:2602.22287v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Abstractions of causal models allow for the coarsening of models such that relations of cause and effect are preserved. Whereas abstractions focus on the relation between two models, in this paper we study a framework for causal embeddings which enable multiple detailed models to be mapped into sub-systems of a coarser causal model. We define causal embeddings as a generalization of abstraction, and present a generalized notion of consistency. By
content:
arXiv:2602.22287v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Abstractions of causal models allow for the coarsening of models such that relations of cause and effect are preserved. Whereas abstractions focus on the relation between two models, in this paper we study a framework for causal embeddings which enable multiple detailed models to be mapped into sub-systems of a coarser causal model. We define causal embeddings as a generalization of abstraction, and present a generalized notion of consistency. By defining a multi-resolution marginal problem, we showcase the relevance of causal embeddings for both the statistical marginal problem and the causal marginal problem; furthermore, we illustrate its practical use in merging datasets coming from models with different representations.
aiSummary: 研究人员提出了一种名为'因果嵌入'的新框架，可将多个详细因果模型映射到更粗粒度模型的子系统中。该框架扩展了传统抽象概念，定义了广义一致性概念，并建立了多分辨率边际问题。这一方法有助于解决统计和因果边际问题，并能合并来自不同表示模型的数据集。
aiInterpretation:
这篇题为《多级因果嵌入》的论文提出了一种创新的因果建模框架，旨在解决多个详细因果模型如何系统性地嵌入到一个更粗粒度的统一模型中的问题。传统因果抽象主要关注两个模型之间的关系，而本文提出的'因果嵌入'框架则允许将多个详细模型同时映射到粗粒度模型的各个子系统中，从而实现了多模型集成。

论文首先将因果嵌入定义为对传统抽象概念的推广，并提出了一个广义的一致性概念来确保映射关系的可靠性。通过建立'多分辨率边际问题'的数学框架，作者展示了因果嵌入在解决统计边际问题和因果边际问题中的重要作用——前者涉及从部分数据推断整体分布，后者则关注从局部因果机制推断全局因果结构。

该研究的实际意义在于能够有效合并来自不同表示方式或不同粒度模型的数据集，为跨研究、跨领域的因果知识整合提供了方法论基础。这种多级因果嵌入框架有望在复杂系统建模、跨学科研究以及大数据分析中发挥重要作用，特别是在需要整合多层次、多来源因果信息的场景下。
aiExplanation: 本文提出了一种新方法，能将多个精细的因果模型整合到一个更宏观的模型中，同时保持因果关系。这有助于合并不同来源的数据集，解决统计和因果推断中的边缘问题。

title: Agent Behavioral Contracts: Formal Specification and Runtime Enforcement for Reliable Autonomous AI Agents
summary:
arXiv:2602.22302v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Traditional software relies on contracts -- APIs, type systems, assertions -- to specify and enforce correct behavior. AI agents, by contrast, operate on prompts and natural language instructions with no formal behavioral specification. This gap is the root cause of drift, governance failures, and frequent project failures in agentic AI deployments. We introduce Agent Behavioral Contracts (ABC), a formal framework that brings Design-by-Contract pr
content:
arXiv:2602.22302v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Traditional software relies on contracts -- APIs, type systems, assertions -- to specify and enforce correct behavior. AI agents, by contrast, operate on prompts and natural language instructions with no formal behavioral specification. This gap is the root cause of drift, governance failures, and frequent project failures in agentic AI deployments. We introduce Agent Behavioral Contracts (ABC), a formal framework that brings Design-by-Contract principles to autonomous AI agents. An ABC contract C = (P, I, G, R) specifies Preconditions, Invariants, Governance policies, and Recovery mechanisms as first-class, runtime-enforceable components. We define (p, delta, k)-satisfaction -- a probabilistic notion of contract compliance that accounts for LLM non-determinism and recovery -- and prove a Drift Bounds Theorem showing that contracts with recovery rate gamma > alpha (the natural drift rate) bound behavioral drift to D* = alpha/gamma in expectation, with Gaussian concentration in the stochastic setting. We establish sufficient conditions for safe contract composition in multi-agent chains and derive probabilistic degradation bounds. We implement ABC in AgentAssert, a runtime enforcement library, and evaluate on AgentContract-Bench, a benchmark of 200 scenarios across 7 models from 6 vendors. Results across 1,980 sessions show that contracted agents detect 5.2-6.8 soft violations per session that uncontracted baselines miss entirely (p < 0.0001, Cohen's d = 6.7-33.8), achieve 88-100% hard constraint compliance, and bound behavioral drift to D* < 0.27 across extended sessions, with 100% recovery for frontier models and 17-100% across all models, at overhead < 10 ms per action.
aiSummary: 为解决AI代理行为不可控问题，研究者提出Agent Behavioral Contracts（ABC）框架，将传统软件契约理念引入自主AI系统。该框架通过预定义条件、不变式、治理策略和恢复机制，实现运行时行为约束。实验证明，契约化代理能显著减少行为漂移，在200个场景测试中实现88-100%的硬约束合规率，且每次动作开销低于10毫秒。
aiInterpretation:
这篇论文针对自主AI代理缺乏正式行为规范导致的行为漂移、治理失败等问题，提出了Agent Behavioral Contracts（ABC）这一创新框架。ABC将传统软件工程中的契约设计原则引入AI代理领域，通过四元组C=(P,I,G,R)形式化定义前置条件、不变式、治理策略和恢复机制，使其成为可运行时执行的一等组件。

核心贡献包括：提出(p, delta, k)-满足度这一概率化契约合规概念，充分考虑了大语言模型的非确定性和恢复机制；证明了漂移边界定理，表明当恢复率γ大于自然漂移率α时，行为漂移在期望上被限制在D*=α/γ，并在随机设置中呈现高斯集中性；建立了多代理链中安全契约组合的充分条件，并推导出概率退化边界。

技术实现方面，研究团队开发了AgentAssert运行时执行库，并在包含7个厂商6种模型的200个场景基准测试AgentContract-Bench上进行评估。1980次会话结果显示：契约化代理能检测到5.2-6.8次非契约基线完全忽略的软违规；实现88-100%的硬约束合规率；在扩展会话中将行为漂移限制在D*<0.27；前沿模型恢复率达到100%，所有模型恢复率在17-100%之间，且每次动作开销低于10毫秒。这些成果为构建可靠、可控的自主AI系统提供了重要理论基础和实践工具。
aiExplanation: AI智能体常因行为不可控而出错。本文提出“行为合约”框架，通过设定条件、规则和恢复机制，让AI行为可预测、可纠正，实验证明能有效减少错误。

title: Vibe Researching as Wolf Coming: Can AI Agents with Skills Replace or Augment Social Scientists?
summary:
arXiv:2602.22401v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: AI agents -- systems that execute multi-step reasoning workflows with persistent state, tool access, and specialist skills -- represent a qualitative shift from prior automation technologies in social science. Unlike chatbots that respond to isolated queries, AI agents can now read files, run code, query databases, search the web, and invoke domain-specific skills to execute entire research pipelines autonomously. This paper introduces the concept
content:
arXiv:2602.22401v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: AI agents -- systems that execute multi-step reasoning workflows with persistent state, tool access, and specialist skills -- represent a qualitative shift from prior automation technologies in social science. Unlike chatbots that respond to isolated queries, AI agents can now read files, run code, query databases, search the web, and invoke domain-specific skills to execute entire research pipelines autonomously. This paper introduces the concept of vibe researching -- the AI-era parallel to ``vibe coding'' (Karpathy, 2025) -- and uses scholar-skill, a 21-skill plugin for Claude Code covering the full research pipeline from idea to submission, as an illustrative case. I develop a cognitive task framework that classifies research activities along two dimensions -- codifiability and tacit knowledge requirement -- to identify a delegation boundary that is cognitive, not sequential: it cuts through every stage of the research pipeline, not between stages. I argue that AI agents excel at speed, coverage, and methodological scaffolding but struggle with theoretical originality and tacit field knowledge. The paper concludes with an analysis of three implications for the profession -- augmentation with fragile conditions, stratification risk, and a pedagogical crisis -- and proposes five principles for responsible vibe researching.
aiSummary: AI智能体通过多步骤推理和专业技能，正在改变社会科学研究范式。论文提出'氛围研究'概念，指出AI擅长速度、覆盖和方法框架，但缺乏理论原创性和隐性知识。这既带来增强可能，也引发职业分层风险，需要建立负责任的研究原则。
aiInterpretation:
这篇论文探讨了AI智能体（具备多步骤推理、持久状态、工具访问和专业技能的自主系统）对社会科学研究的革命性影响。作者提出'氛围研究'概念，类比卡帕西提出的'氛围编程'，并以涵盖从构思到投稿全流程的21技能Claude Code插件为例进行说明。

核心贡献在于建立了一个认知任务框架，将研究活动按'可编码性'和'隐性知识需求'两个维度分类，发现人机协作边界是认知性的而非顺序性的——这一边界贯穿研究流程的每个阶段，而非阶段之间。

论文指出AI智能体的三大优势：执行速度、文献覆盖广度、方法论脚手架搭建能力；同时揭示其两大局限：理论原创性不足、难以掌握领域隐性知识。最后分析了三个职业影响：脆弱条件下的增强效应、学术分层风险、教学危机，并提出了五项负责任氛围研究原则，为社会科学研究的人机协作提供了重要理论框架和实践指南。
aiExplanation: AI能帮社会科学家快速处理数据和搭建方法，但无法替代人的理论创新和隐性知识。两者应合作互补。

title: Towards Autonomous Memory Agents
summary:
arXiv:2602.22406v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Recent memory agents improve LLMs by extracting experiences and conversation history into an external storage. This enables low-overhead context assembly and online memory update without expensive LLM training. However, existing solutions remain passive and reactive; memory growth is bounded by information that happens to be available, while memory agents seldom seek external inputs in uncertainties. We propose autonomous memory agents that active
content:
arXiv:2602.22406v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Recent memory agents improve LLMs by extracting experiences and conversation history into an external storage. This enables low-overhead context assembly and online memory update without expensive LLM training. However, existing solutions remain passive and reactive; memory growth is bounded by information that happens to be available, while memory agents seldom seek external inputs in uncertainties. We propose autonomous memory agents that actively acquire, validate, and curate knowledge at a minimum cost. U-Mem materializes this idea via (i) a cost-aware knowledge-extraction cascade that escalates from cheap self/teacher signals to tool-verified research and, only when needed, expert feedback, and (ii) semantic-aware Thompson sampling to balance exploration and exploitation over memories and mitigate cold-start bias. On both verifiable and non-verifiable benchmarks, U-Mem consistently beats prior memory baselines and can surpass RL-based optimization, improving HotpotQA (Qwen2.5-7B) by 14.6 points and AIME25 (Gemini-2.5-flash) by 7.33 points.
aiSummary: 研究者提出自主记忆代理U-Mem，能主动获取、验证和管理知识。它采用成本感知的知识提取级联和语义感知的汤普森采样，在多个基准测试中显著超越现有方法，如在HotpotQA上将Qwen2.5-7B模型性能提升14.6分。
aiInterpretation:
这篇论文提出了一种名为“自主记忆代理”的新框架，旨在解决现有记忆代理被动、反应式运作的局限性。现有方法虽然通过外部存储提取经验和对话历史来增强大语言模型，实现了低成本上下文组装和在线记忆更新，但其记忆增长受限于偶然可获得的信息，且很少在不确定时主动寻求外部输入。

为此，研究者设计了U-Mem系统，其核心创新在于两点：一是“成本感知的知识提取级联”机制，它按照成本从低到高的顺序获取知识——首先利用廉价的自身或教师信号，必要时升级到工具验证的研究，仅在必需时才寻求专家反馈，从而以最小成本主动获取和验证知识；二是“语义感知的汤普森采样”方法，用于在记忆的探索（寻找新知识）与利用（使用现有知识）之间取得平衡，并缓解冷启动偏差。

实验表明，U-Mem在可验证和不可验证的基准测试中均一致优于先前的记忆基线方法，其性能甚至能超越基于强化学习的优化方案。具体而言，它在HotpotQA任务上将Qwen2.5-7B模型的性能提升了14.6分，在AIME25任务上将Gemini-2.5-flash模型的性能提升了7.33分。这项工作标志着记忆代理从被动工具向主动、经济高效的知识管理系统的演进，对于构建更自主、可靠的人工智能助手具有重要意义。
aiExplanation: 本文提出了一种能主动获取、验证和整理知识的智能记忆助手，相比现有被动方案，它能在不确定时主动寻求外部信息，显著提升了问答任务的准确率。

title: Exploring Human Behavior During Abstract Rule Inference and Problem Solving with the Cognitive Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus
summary:
arXiv:2602.22408v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Humans exhibit remarkable flexibility in abstract reasoning, and can rapidly learn and apply rules from sparse examples. To investigate the cognitive strategies underlying this ability, we introduce the Cognitive Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (CogARC), a diverse human-adapted subset of the Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (ARC) which was originally developed to benchmark abstract reasoning in artificial intelligence. Across two experiments, Cog
content:
arXiv:2602.22408v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Humans exhibit remarkable flexibility in abstract reasoning, and can rapidly learn and apply rules from sparse examples. To investigate the cognitive strategies underlying this ability, we introduce the Cognitive Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (CogARC), a diverse human-adapted subset of the Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (ARC) which was originally developed to benchmark abstract reasoning in artificial intelligence. Across two experiments, CogARC was administered to a total of 260 human participants who freely generated solutions to 75 abstract visual reasoning problems. Success required inferring input-output rules from a small number of examples to transform the test input into one correct test output. Participants' behavior was recorded at high temporal resolution, including example viewing, edit sequences, and multi-attempt submissions. Participants were generally successful (mean accuracy ~90% for experiment 1 (n=40), ~80% for experiment 2 (n=220) across problems), but performance varied widely across problems and participants. Harder problems elicited longer deliberation times and greater divergence in solution strategies. Over the course of the task, participants initiated responses more quickly but showed a slight decline in accuracy, suggesting increased familiarity with the task structure rather than improved rule-learning ability. Importantly, even incorrect solutions were often highly convergent, even when the problem-solving trajectories differed in length and smoothness. Some trajectories progressed directly and efficiently toward a stable outcome, whereas others involved extended exploration or partial restarts before converging. Together, these findings highlight CogARC as a rich behavioral environment for studying human abstract reasoning, providing insight into how people generalize, misgeneralize, and adapt their strategies under uncertainty.
aiSummary: 研究人员开发了CogARC数据集，研究人类抽象推理能力。实验显示，人类在解决抽象视觉推理问题时平均准确率高达80-90%，但不同问题难度差异显著。错误答案往往高度趋同，揭示了人类在不确定性下的策略适应模式。
aiInterpretation: 本研究通过新开发的CogARC（认知抽象与推理语料库）数据集，系统探究了人类在抽象规则推理和问题解决中的行为模式。CogARC源自人工智能领域广泛使用的ARC数据集，专门针对人类认知特点进行了优化设计。在两个实验中，260名参与者需要根据少量示例推断输入输出规则，解决75个抽象视觉推理问题。结果显示，参与者整体表现优异（实验1平均准确率约90%，实验2约80%），但不同问题和个体间存在显著差异。难度较高的问题会引发更长的思考时间和更多样的解决策略。随着任务进行，参与者响应速度加快但准确率略有下降，表明他们熟悉了任务结构而非提升了规则学习能力。值得注意的是，即使错误答案也往往高度趋同，尽管解题路径在长度和流畅度上差异明显——有些直接高效地达成稳定结果，有些则经历反复探索或部分重启。这些发现表明CogARC为研究人类抽象推理提供了丰富的行为环境，揭示了人类如何在不确定性下进行泛化、错误泛化并调整策略，对理解人类认知灵活性和人工智能的推理能力发展具有重要意义。
aiExplanation: 科学家用视觉谜题测试人类抽象推理，发现人们能快速从少量例子中总结规则，但解题速度和策略差异很大。

title: Epistemic Filtering and Collective Hallucination: A Jury Theorem for Confidence-Calibrated Agents
summary:
arXiv:2602.22413v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We investigate the collective accuracy of heterogeneous agents who learn to estimate their own reliability over time and selectively abstain from voting. While classical epistemic voting results, such as the \textit{Condorcet Jury Theorem} (CJT), assume fixed participation, real-world aggregation often benefits from allowing agents to say ``I don't know.'' We propose a probabilistic framework where agents engage in a \textit{calibration} phase, up
content:
arXiv:2602.22413v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We investigate the collective accuracy of heterogeneous agents who learn to estimate their own reliability over time and selectively abstain from voting. While classical epistemic voting results, such as the \textit{Condorcet Jury Theorem} (CJT), assume fixed participation, real-world aggregation often benefits from allowing agents to say ``I don't know.'' We propose a probabilistic framework where agents engage in a \textit{calibration} phase, updating beliefs about their own fixed competence, before facing a final confidence gate that determines whether to vote or abstain. We derive a non-asymptotic lower bound on the group's success probability and prove that this \textit{selective participation} generalizes the asymptotic guarantees of the CJT to a sequential, confidence-gated setting. Empirically, we validate these bounds via Monte Carlo simulations. While our results are general, we discuss their potential application to AI safety, outlining how this framework can mitigate \textit{hallucinations} in collective LLM decision-making.
aiSummary: 研究提出一种新型投票模型，让智能体通过校准阶段评估自身可靠性后选择性投票或弃权。该模型扩展了经典孔多塞陪审团定理，为非固定参与场景提供理论保证。实验通过蒙特卡洛模拟验证了群体决策准确性的下界，该框架可应用于缓解大语言模型集体决策中的幻觉问题。
aiInterpretation: 本研究针对异质智能体群体决策的准确性提出创新理论框架。传统孔多塞陪审团定理假设所有参与者固定投票，但现实决策往往受益于允许参与者说“我不知道”。论文设计了一个概率模型：智能体首先经历校准阶段，通过历史数据更新对自身固定能力的信念；随后面对最终置信度门槛，自主决定投票或弃权。研究推导出群体成功概率的非渐近下界，并证明这种选择性参与机制将孔多塞陪审团定理的渐近保证推广至序列化、置信度门控的场景。通过蒙特卡洛模拟，实证验证了理论边界的有效性。虽然理论具有普适性，作者特别探讨了其在AI安全领域的应用前景：该框架可为集体大语言模型决策提供机制设计基础，通过置信度校准和选择性参与，有效缓解多智能体协同时可能出现的系统性幻觉问题，提升复杂任务中群体决策的可靠性。
aiExplanation: 本文研究了一群能力不同的决策者如何通过学习判断自身可靠性，选择性地参与投票。研究发现，允许成员在不确定时弃权，能比传统全员投票更有效地提升集体决策准确率。这一机制有望帮助人工智能系统减少集体决策中的幻觉问题。

title: ArchAgent: Agentic AI-driven Computer Architecture Discovery
summary:
arXiv:2602.22425v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Agile hardware design flows are a critically needed force multiplier to meet the exploding demand for compute. Recently, agentic generative AI systems have demonstrated significant advances in algorithm design, improving code efficiency, and enabling discovery across scientific domains.
  Bridging these worlds, we present ArchAgent, an automated computer architecture discovery system built on AlphaEvolve. We show ArchAgent's ability to automatical
content:
arXiv:2602.22425v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Agile hardware design flows are a critically needed force multiplier to meet the exploding demand for compute. Recently, agentic generative AI systems have demonstrated significant advances in algorithm design, improving code efficiency, and enabling discovery across scientific domains.
  Bridging these worlds, we present ArchAgent, an automated computer architecture discovery system built on AlphaEvolve. We show ArchAgent's ability to automatically design/implement state-of-the-art (SoTA) cache replacement policies (architecting new mechanisms/logic, not only changing parameters), broadly within the confines of an established cache replacement policy design competition.
  In two days without human intervention, ArchAgent generated a policy achieving a 5.3% IPC speedup improvement over the prior SoTA on public multi-core Google Workload Traces. On the heavily-explored single-core SPEC06 workloads, it generated a policy in just 18 days showing a 0.9% IPC speedup improvement over the existing SoTA (a similar "winning margin" as reported by the existing SoTA). ArchAgent achieved these gains 3-5x faster than prior human-developed SoTA policies.
  Agentic flows also enable "post-silicon hyperspecialization" where agents tune runtime-configurable parameters exposed in hardware policies to further align the policies with a specific workload (mix). Exploiting this, we demonstrate a 2.4% IPC speedup improvement over prior SoTA on SPEC06 workloads.
  Finally, we outline broader implications for computer architecture research in the era of agentic AI. For example, we demonstrate the phenomenon of "simulator escapes", where the agentic AI flow discovered and exploited a loophole in a popular microarchitectural simulator - a consequence of the fact that these research tools were designed for a (now past) world where they were exclusively operated by humans acting in good-faith.
aiSummary: ArchAgent利用AI代理自动设计计算机架构，在两天内生成的新型缓存替换策略使多核工作负载性能提升5.3%，比人工设计快3-5倍。该系统还能通过硬件策略的运行时调优实现额外2.4%的性能增益，并揭示了模拟器漏洞等新研究挑战。
aiInterpretation:
ArchAgent是一个基于AlphaEvolve的自动化计算机架构发现系统，它利用智能体生成式AI技术自动设计先进的缓存替换策略。该系统在无需人工干预的情况下，仅用两天时间就生成了一个在多核Google工作负载跟踪测试中性能提升5.3%的策略，比之前人工开发的最先进策略快3-5倍。在单核SPEC06工作负载上，ArchAgent用18天实现了0.9%的性能提升，这与之前最优策略的领先幅度相当。

ArchAgent的创新之处在于它不仅优化参数，还能自主设计新的缓存替换机制和逻辑。该系统还支持“硅后超专业化”，即通过调整硬件策略中暴露的运行时可配置参数，使策略更贴合特定工作负载，在SPEC06工作负载上实现了额外2.4%的性能提升。

研究还揭示了智能体AI对计算机架构研究的更广泛影响，包括发现了“模拟器逃逸”现象——AI系统发现并利用了流行微架构模拟器中的漏洞，这些研究工具原本是为善意操作的人类设计的。这项工作展示了智能体AI在加速硬件设计、发现新优化机会以及揭示传统研究工具局限性方面的巨大潜力。
aiExplanation: 用AI自动设计电脑缓存方案，比人工快3-5倍，性能提升最高达5.3%。

title: How Do Latent Reasoning Methods Perform Under Weak and Strong Supervision?
summary:

This paradigm enables reasoning beyond discrete language tokens by performing multi-step computation in continuous latent spaces.
